headlineSummary: Microsoft Copilot and Anthropic drew similarly negative discussion with a close tie and medium evidence, while OpenAI was more mixed with limited evidence [E1][E3][E4][E5][E6][E7].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot was viewed negatively, with criticism focused on the official Discord moderation and doubts that it needs a community server [E1][E2].

Anthropic was also viewed negatively in a close tie, driven by complaints about Cowork creating a large VM bundle, trust concerns, and performance/functionality issues, though one comment praised Claude Code productivity [E3][E4][E5][E6].
OpenAI was mixed overall, with limited evidence: one comment praised its sandbox approach compared with Anthropic, while another criticized ChatGPT for a poor benchmarking suggestion [E4][E7].

evidence.excerpt: “Microslop” filtered in the official Microsoft Copilot Discord server

annotations.rationale: The title frames Microsoft Copilot around filtering a derogatory term in its official server, implying criticism of moderation/policy behavior.

evidence.excerpt: What community is there to house around Microsoft Copilot? Seriously, why does Microsoft Copilot need a Discord Server?

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly questions whether Microsoft Copilot has a meaningful community or need for a Discord server, expressing skepticism.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic Cowork feature creates 10GB VM bundle on macOS without warning

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly names Anthropic and presents Cowork as creating a large VM bundle 'without warning,' which is negative product framing.

evidence.excerpt: It makes it so I can't use the Claude Cowork within my VMs and that's when I found out it was running a VM, because it caused a nested VM error. All it does is limit functionality, add extra space and cause lag. A better sandbox environment would be Apple seatbelt, which is what OpenAI uses

annotations.rationale: The commenter criticizes Claude Cowork for VM-related limitations, disk use, and lag.
OpenAI is presented as using a 'better sandbox environment,' a mild favorable comparison versus Anthropic's approach.

evidence.excerpt: Why would I ever trust a vibe coded analysis? How do I, a non expert in this niche, know that Opus isn't pulling a fast one on both of us? LLMs write convincing bullshit that even fools experts.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly distrusts Opus and says LLMs produce convincing falsehoods, a strong criticism of reliability.

evidence.excerpt: I feel extremely productive with a single Claude Code for a project.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Claude Code makes them 'extremely productive,' which is strong praise for the product.

evidence.excerpt: Of course chatgpt suggested I benchmark an Intel Mac vs. newer apple silicon. Thanks chatgpt, there's a reason people still hate AI.

annotations.rationale: The commenter blames ChatGPT for a poor suggestion and uses it as an example of why people dislike AI, indicating negative sentiment.
